inputs.userGoal: Create a real-time document Q&A API leveraging Azure OpenAI and Azure Cosmos DB, expose endpoints using Azure API Management, and enforce rate limits per user.

architecturePrinciples: Secure by default with managed identity
Per-user rate limiting at the API gateway
Separation of concerns: API gateway → backend → AI + data

resources.name: oai-docqa-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Azure OpenAI provides managed GPT models for document Q&A with enterprise security, SLA, and content filtering. S0 is the standard production SKU.
alternativesConsidered: Azure AI Foundry (AIServices kind) — broader multi-service account but unnecessary for a single OpenAI workload
Self-hosted open-source LLM on AKS — higher operational overhead and no managed content safety
reasoning.tradeoffs: S0 SKU has token-per-minute rate limits; burst traffic may require provisioned throughput units. Managed service reduces ops burden at the cost of less model customization.

resources.dependencyReasoning: No upstream dependencies; foundational AI resource.

resources.name: cosmos-docqa-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Cosmos DB NoSQL API provides low-latency reads for document storage and Q&A conversation history. Serverless mode is cost-effective for variable traffic patterns. Session consistency balances performance and read-your-writes guarantees.
alternativesConsidered: Azure SQL Database — relational model not ideal for semi-structured document storage
Azure Blob Storage — no built-in querying or indexing for document metadata
reasoning.tradeoffs: Serverless is single-region only and has a 1 GB max document size per partition. No shared-throughput databases. Suitable for dev/early production; switch to provisioned throughput for predictable high-volume workloads.

resources.dependencyReasoning: No upstream dependencies; foundational data resource.

resources.name: asp-docqa-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Basic B1 Linux plan is the minimum production-capable tier supporting custom domains and always-on. Keeps costs low for a single-API workload while allowing scale-up if needed.
alternativesConsidered: Consumption Functions (Y1) — lower cost but cold starts impact real-time Q&A latency
Container Apps — more operational flexibility but overkill for a single API service
reasoning.tradeoffs: B1 does not support deployment slots or autoscale; upgrade to S1 (Standard) if those features are needed later.

resources.dependencyReasoning: No upstream dependencies; compute hosting layer.

resources.name: app-docqa-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: App Service hosts the Q&A API backend that orchestrates calls to Azure OpenAI and Cosmos DB. Python 3.12 is the most common runtime for AI/LLM workloads with strong SDK support. Always-on prevents cold starts for real-time responses.
alternativesConsidered: Azure Functions — event-driven but cold starts are problematic for real-time APIs
Azure Container Apps — more flexible but adds container image management overhead
reasoning.tradeoffs: Tied to a single App Service Plan instance; scaling requires plan upgrade. Simpler deployment model than containers.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires the App Service Plan for hosting, Azure OpenAI for completions, and Cosmos DB for document storage and retrieval.

resources.name: apim-docqa-prod-001

rateLimitPolicy.description: Per-user rate limiting enforced via APIM policy using rate-limit-by-key with subscription key or JWT claim as the counter key.

reasoning.whyChosen: API Management Consumption tier provides serverless API gateway with built-in rate-limit-by-key policy for per-user throttling. No infrastructure to manage and pay-per-call pricing aligns with variable traffic. Supports subscription keys, JWT validation, and OAuth2 for user identity.
alternativesConsidered: StandardV2 APIM — more features (built-in cache, VNet integration) but higher baseline cost
Azure Front Door with rate limiting — L7 load balancer but no API-level policy management
reasoning.tradeoffs: Consumption tier has no built-in cache, no VNet support, and a cold-start latency (~1-2s on first request). 10,000 requests/subscription/month included free; costs scale linearly after. Upgrade to BasicV2 or StandardV2 for production workloads with sustained traffic.

resources.dependencyReasoning: APIM routes requests to the App Service backend; must be provisioned after the backend is available.

overallReasoning.summary: This architecture uses a serverless-leaning approach: Azure OpenAI handles document Q&A completions, Cosmos DB (serverless) stores documents and conversation history, an App Service hosts the orchestration API, and API Management (Consumption) exposes endpoints with per-user rate limiting via rate-limit-by-key policy. All resources deploy to eastus in a single resource group.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Consumption-tier APIM and serverless Cosmos DB optimize for cost at the expense of cold-start latency and single-region only. The B1 App Service Plan is entry-level production; upgrade to S1+ for deployment slots and autoscale. No VNet isolation in this default plan — add Private Endpoints and VNet integration for stricter security requirements.

plan.validation: All resources deploy to eastus. Cosmos DB serverless is single-region (consistent with single-location config). APIM Consumption does not require VNet. App Service B1 supports always-on and custom domains. OpenAI S0 is the standard paid tier. No conflicting pairing constraints detected.
